title: A general proof of integer Rényi QNEC
abstract: The Rényi quantum null energy condition conjectures that the second null shape variation of the sandwiched Rényi divergence (SRD) of an excited state relative to the vacuum is non-negative in local Poincaré-invariant quantum field theory, giving a one-parameter generalization of the quantum null energy condition (QNEC). We prove Rényi QNEC for all integer Rényi parameters $n\geq 2$ for von Neumann algebras carrying a half-sided modular inclusion structure. The only assumption on the excited state is finiteness of its SRD relative to the vacuum. Concretely, for any $σ$-finite von Neumann algebra with such an inclusion, we prove log-convexity, under the associated null-translation semigroup, of the Kosaki $L^n$ norm of any normal positive functional with finite $L^n$ norm.
summary: This theoretical paper proves the Rényi Quantum Null Energy Condition for integer parameters $n \ge 2$ within the framework of local Poincaré-invariant Quantum Field Theory. It achieves this by demonstrating the log-convexity of the Rényi relative entropy using sophisticated tools from operator algebras and non-commutative functional analysis. The result provides a powerful mathematical constraint on the structure of quantum states in QFT.
main_problem: The paper aims to prove the Rényi Quantum Null Energy Condition (Rényi QNEC), which is a generalization of the standard Quantum Null Energy Condition (QNEC). This conjecture relates to the non-negativity of the second null shape variation of the Sandwiched Rényi Divergence (SRD).
main_result: The authors prove the Rényi QNEC for all integer Rényi parameters $n \ge 2$ for von Neumann algebras possessing a half-sided modular inclusion structure. This is achieved by proving the log-convexity of the associated Kosaki $L_n$ norm.
method: The proof employs advanced techniques from operator algebras, specifically utilizing von Neumann algebras, Haagerup's non-commutative $L_p$ spaces, and analyzing the dynamics generated by null translations via semigroups.
model_or_system: The context is local Poincaré-invariant Quantum Field Theory (QFT) on Minkowski spacetime, analyzed using null coordinates and associated with von Neumann algebras defined over null-cut regions.
key_observables: Sandwiched Rényi Divergence (SRD), $D_n(\Psi\|\Omega; M_A)$, and the Kosaki $L_n$ norm of normal positive functionals.
important_parameters: Integer Rényi parameter $n \ge 2$, and the continuous parameter $c$ governing the null translation flow.
main_assumptions: The existence of a half-sided modular inclusion (HSMI) structure on the von Neumann algebras, and the finiteness of the SRD of the excited state relative to the vacuum.
figures_summary: Figure 1 schematically illustrates null cut algebras, such as the Rindler wedge and the null cut algebra $M_b$.
paper_structure: The paper builds from defining the SRD and the RQNEC conjecture, establishing the necessary mathematical framework using non-commutative $L_p$ spaces and null translations, and culminating in the proof of log-convexity via a cyclic Ward identity.
why_interesting: The rigorous mathematical framework connecting quantum energy conditions (a fundamental constraint in QFT) to the convexity properties of generalized entropy measures (Rényi divergences) is highly relevant for understanding quantum field theory constraints in curved or null-geometry backgrounds.
analysis_basis: full PDF text, analyzed in chunks

figures.caption: Figure 1. Schematic of the null cut algebras. The transverse directions are perpendicular to the plane of the figure. The null planes x± = 0 are labelled. Light blue regions correspond to the Rindler wedge R : {x+ > 0, x−< 0} and its commutant R′. Dark blue regions correspond to the null cut algebra Mb and its commutant M ′ b.
